framing.overarching_premise: The player is a lone survivor — tribe lost, location unknown, skills untested. The valley is not a destination. It is a crucible. Four encounters force the player to observe, adapt, and make decisions under pressure. By the end, the player does not just survive — they understand the valley's logic. That understanding is the only currency that matters here.
framing.narrative_voice: sparse, observational, never explains — trusts the player to interpret

intro.speaker: NARRATOR
intro.text: The water hole is dangerous at dusk. Every creature in the valley comes to drink — and not all of them are prey.

mid_observe.speaker: NARRATOR
mid_observe.text: Count them. Three... no, four. They drink in turns. The largest goes first. Remember that.

complete.speaker: NARRATOR
complete.text: You made it out. The valley noticed. It always notices.

fail.speaker: NARRATOR
fail.text: Too slow. Too loud. The valley does not forgive mistakes — it only offers them again.

intro.speaker: NPC_ELDER
intro.text: Follow the herd. They know where the water is. They know where the predators hunt. A lone human cannot survive here — but a human who watches and learns from the great beasts? That human has a chance.

mid_valley.speaker: NARRATOR
mid_valley.text: The ground shakes with each step. You feel it through your feet before you hear it. This is what the valley sounded like before humans arrived. This is what it will sound like after.

complete.speaker: NPC_ELDER
complete.text: You came back. Not many do, after their first night alone. Sit. Eat. Then tell me what you saw out there — every detail matters. The patterns of this land are the only map we have.

intro.speaker: NARRATOR
intro.text: The herd moves south. Three days ago, this clearing was full of smaller animals. Now it is empty. Something scared them off — and it is still here.

mid_examine.speaker: NARRATOR
mid_examine.text: Claw marks. Fresh. Three toes — narrow spacing. They were running. Not hunting. Running from something larger.

escape.speaker: NPC_SCOUT
escape.text: Do not run. Running tells the predator you are prey. Stand still. Let it decide you are not worth the effort. It is the hardest thing you will ever do — and the most important.

intro.speaker: NARRATOR
intro.text: The valley remembers everything. Every footprint in the mud, every broken branch, every silence where there should be sound. Learn to read it, and you will survive. Ignore it, and the valley will swallow you whole.

observe.speaker: NARRATOR
observe.text: Do not move. They have not seen you yet. Watch how the young ones stay at the centre of the group. The adults form a wall. They have been doing this for longer than your tribe has existed.

retreat.speaker: NARRATOR
retreat.text: Back away. Slowly. One step at a time. You have seen enough. Some knowledge costs more than it is worth.

NPC_ELDER.personality: pragmatic, weathered, economical with words — speaks only when information has survival value
NPC_ELDER.speech_pattern: short declarative sentences, no metaphor, no comfort — only fact and instruction

NPC_SCOUT.personality: young, observant, nervous energy — has survived by being faster than everyone else
NPC_SCOUT.speech_pattern: rapid delivery, interrupts self, corrects mid-sentence — thinking aloud

NARRATOR.personality: the valley itself — observational, non-judgmental, ancient
NARRATOR.speech_pattern: present tense, second person ('you'), no comfort — describes what is, not what should be

dialogue_system_notes.audio_integration: Audio Agent #16 should attach voice_asset URLs to corresponding NarrDialogue_* actors as AudioComponent with attenuation radius matching trigger radius
dialogue_system_notes.quest_integration: QuestTrigger_* actors from Agent #14 are the entry points — NarrDialogue_* actors fire on proximity within the quest zone
